title: Introduction to zigdist
text: zigdist package provides high-performance, minimal-dependency implementations distance metrics Zig programming language. currently implements: Euclidean Distance (self-distance cross-distance numeric matrices/data frames). Gower (1971) Distance (self-distance cross-distance mixed-type data frames containing numeric, categorical, ordinal features).

title: Introduction to zigdist
text: calculate pairwise Euclidean distance matrix numeric matrix x: calculate cross-distance matrix two matrices x (size N×PN \times P) y (size M×PM \times P):

title: Introduction to zigdist
text: Gower distance designed mixed data types. Numeric ordinal columns scaled range: dijk=|xik−xjk|range(x⋅k)d_{ijk} = \frac{|x_{ik} - x_{jk}|}{\text{range}(x_{\cdot k})} Nominal (categorical/logical) columns compared using simple matching: dijk=(xik≠xjk)d_{ijk} = (x_{ik} \neq x_{jk}) total Gower distance weighted average components: Dij=∑kwkdijk∑kwkD_{ij} = \frac{\sum_k w_k d_{ijk}}{\sum_k w_k}

title: Introduction to zigdist
text: Ordinal factors natively supported. Following Podani’s (1999) metric approach, zd_gower automatically converts ordered factors integer ranks (1,2,…1, 2, \dots) handles range-scaled numeric columns. preserves relative ordering “closeness” categories (e.g., distance Low Medium smaller Low High), matching R’s standard cluster::daisy behavior.

title: Introduction to zigdist
text: can also compute cross-distances two mixed-type datasets, provide custom column weights: Columns zero range (.e. constant values) automatically ignored (excluded calculations) without raising division--zero errors.
code: # Create a mixed-type data frame with numeric, nominal, and ordinal columns df_x <- data.frame(   a = c(1, 2, 5),   b = factor(c("A", "B", "A")),   c = ordered(c("Low", "Medium", "High"), levels = c("Low", "Medium", "High")) )  # Calculate pairwise Gower distances zd_gower(df_x) ##           [,1]      [,2]      [,3] ## [1,] 0.0000000 0.5833333 0.6666667 ## [2,] 0.5833333 0.0000000 0.7500000 ## [3,] 0.6666667 0.7500000 0.0000000 df_y <- data.frame(   a = c(2, 4),   b = factor(c("A", "C")),   c = ordered(c("Medium", "High"), levels = c("Low", "Medium", "High")) )  # Weighted Gower cross-distance zd_gower(df_x, df_y, weight = c(2.0, 0.5, 0.5)) ##           [,1]      [,2] ## [1,] 0.2500000 0.8333333 ## [2,] 0.1666667 0.5833333 ## [3,] 0.5833333 0.3333333

title: Introduction to zigdist
text: Gower, J. C. (1971). general coefficient similarity properties. Biometrics, 27(4), 857–871. https://doi.org/10.2307/2528823 Podani, J. (1999). Extending Gower’s general coefficient similarity ordinal characters. Taxon, 48(2), 331–340. https://doi.org/10.2307/1224024

what: High-Performance Distance Metric Calculations using Zig
title: High-Performance Distance Metric Calculations using Zig
text: zigdist experimental demonstration integrates Zig programming language R package. provides high-performance distance metric calculations proof--concept. Zig currently supported CRAN, exceptional performance, simple FFI, data-oriented design, robust systems programming capabilities suggest significant interest R package development community.

title: High-Performance Distance Metric Calculations using Zig
text: Euclidean (zd_euclidean()): Pairwise row distance cross-distance numeric matrices, supporting missing values (partial distance scaling) Gower (zd_gower()): Mixed-type distance computations (scaling numeric features range, matching categorical features), supporting missing values (pairwise deletion) distance methods use multi-threaded parallel execution specifying num_threads > 1.

title: High-Performance Distance Metric Calculations using Zig
text: can install development version zigdist GitHub: Note: Zig compiler (v0.13.0 later) must PATH compile package source. recommended way install manage Zig versions using zvm (Zig Version Manager). 1. Install zvm script install zvm ~/.zvm add necessary environment variables shell profile (e.g., .bashrc, .zshrc). might need restart terminal source shell profile changes take effect. 2. Install Zig version zvm installed, can install current stable Zig version: can also set default version zvm alias default stable.
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("brownag/zigdist") curl -fsSL https://www.zvm.app/install.sh | bash zvm install stable zvm use stable

title: High-Performance Distance Metric Calculations using Zig
High-Performance Distance Metric Calculations using Zig
text: demonstrate functional equivalence, verify outputs zigdist match standard R implementations (stats::dist cluster::daisy) using .equal():
code: # Euclidean vs. stats::dist x_check <- matrix(rnorm(300), nrow = 20, ncol = 15) all.equal(zd_euclidean(x_check), as.matrix(dist(x_check)), check.attributes = FALSE) ## [1] TRUE # Gower vs. cluster::daisy library(cluster) df_check <- data.frame(   n = rnorm(20),   f = factor(sample(letters[1:3], 20, replace = TRUE)),   c = factor(sample(c("Yes", "No"), 20, replace = TRUE)) ) all.equal(zd_gower(df_check), as.matrix(daisy(df_check, metric = "gower")), check.attributes = FALSE) ## [1] TRUE

title: High-Performance Distance Metric Calculations using Zig
text: benchmarks comparing zigdist standard R implementations, using microbenchmark package.

title: High-Performance Distance Metric Calculations using Zig
text: compare zigdist::zd_euclidean (single-threaded multi-threaded) R’s built-stats::dist (converted full matrix) 500 100 numeric matrix. Even single thread (num_threads = 1L), zigdist significantly outperforms R’s native stats::dist (written optimized C) 1.7x. Spawning multiple threads (e.g., num_threads = 4L) achieves 4.3x speedup.
code: set.seed(42) x_bench <- matrix(rnorm(50000), nrow = 500, ncol = 100)  bench_eucl <- microbenchmark(   `zigdist 1 thread` = zd_euclidean(x_bench, num_threads = 1L),   `zigdist 4 thread` = zd_euclidean(x_bench, num_threads = 4L),   stats_dist = as.matrix(dist(x_bench)),   times = 50 ) print(bench_eucl) ## Unit: milliseconds ##              expr       min        lq      mean    median        uq       max ##  zigdist 1 thread  6.429038  7.427399 11.090695  8.315266 15.527293 18.913611 ##  zigdist 4 thread  2.526398  3.016101  4.354398  3.385486  6.104136  9.398731 ##        stats_dist 13.214829 13.844654 19.579176 14.417614 28.740439 40.654006 ##  neval ##     50 ##     50 ##     50

title: High-Performance Distance Metric Calculations using Zig
text: compare zigdist::zd_gower (single-threaded multi-threaded) cluster::daisy(..., metric = "gower") (converted full matrix) mixed-type data frame 500 rows 6 columns (3 numeric, 3 categorical). mixed-type data, zigdist represents substantial improvement cluster::daisy, calculating distances 7.5x faster single-threaded mode. smaller calculations, running sequentially single thread recommended eliminate thread-spawning overhead.
code: set.seed(42) N_bench <- 500 df_bench <- data.frame(   n1 = rnorm(N_bench),   n2 = runif(N_bench),   n3 = rnorm(N_bench, mean = 10, sd = 2),   c1 = factor(sample(letters[1:4], N_bench, replace = TRUE)),   c2 = factor(sample(c("Yes", "No"), N_bench, replace = TRUE)),   c3 = factor(sample(colors()[1:10], N_bench, replace = TRUE)) )  bench_gow <- microbenchmark(   `zigdist 1 thread` = zd_gower(df_bench, num_threads = 1L),   `zigdist 4 thread` = zd_gower(df_bench, num_threads = 4L),   cluster_daisy = as.matrix(daisy(df_bench, metric = "gower")),   times = 20 ) print(bench_gow) ## Unit: microseconds ##              expr      min       lq      mean    median        uq       max ##  zigdist 1 thread 1124.568 1192.451  2290.963  1729.693  1867.452  8402.135 ##  zigdist 4 thread  617.543 1050.959  1771.018  1579.399  1892.815  7103.058 ##     cluster_daisy 7315.448 9224.679 11691.348 12963.284 13780.539 16096.776 ##  neval ##     20 ##     20 ##     20

title: Compute Pairwise Euclidean Distance — zd_euclidean
text: Computes pairwise Euclidean distance matrix rows matrix x, cross-distance matrix rows x rows y. Supports missing values (NA) using partial distance scaling.

title: Compute Pairwise Euclidean Distance — zd_euclidean
text: x numeric matrix data frame size N_x x P. y optional numeric matrix data frame size N_y x P. num_threads integer specifying number threads use. Defaults 1L (single-threaded).

title: Compute Pairwise Euclidean Distance — zd_euclidean
text: numeric matrix distances. y NULL, returns   N_x x N_x matrix. y provided, returns N_x x N_y matrix.

title: Compute Gower Distance — zd_gower
text: Computes Gower (1971) distance matrix mixed-type datasets. Supports missing values (NA) setting weight contribution zero.

title: Compute Gower Distance — zd_gower
code: zd_gower(x, y = NULL, weight = NULL, num_threads = 1L)

title: Compute Gower Distance — zd_gower
text: x data frame matrix size N_x x P. y optional data frame matrix size N_y x P. weight optional numeric vector weights column. NULL, columns weighted equally. num_threads integer specifying number threads use. Defaults 1L (single-threaded).

title: Compute Gower Distance — zd_gower
text: numeric matrix Gower distances. y NULL, returns   N_x x N_x matrix. y provided, returns N_x x N_y matrix.

title: Compute Gower Distance — zd_gower
text: Gower, J. C. (1971). general coefficient similarity properties. Biometrics, 27(4), 857–871. doi:10.2307/2528823 Podani, J. (1999). Extending Gower's general coefficient similarity ordinal characters. Taxon, 48(2), 331–340. doi:10.2307/1224024
